title: Cache D1 /components/list responses in KV (HTML + JSON, SWR)
description: Caches D1 componentslist responses in KV for both HTML and JSON formats to reduce repeated query work and improve response times.
body:
### Motivation
- Reduce repeated D1 query work for `/components/list` by caching both HTML and JSON representations in KV so subsequent reloads are fast. 
- Avoid collisions between HTML and JSON cache entries for the same pathname+query by differentiating representations.

### Description
- Pass the shared `CacheService` and `ExecutionContext` into `tryD1Route` and use a new `handleD1ComponentsListWithCache` to serve and populate KV entries with `HIT`/`MISS`/`STALE` semantics. 
- Introduce representation-aware cache keys by appending a `__format` query param (`json` or `html`) via `getD1RepresentationCacheUrl` so HTML and JSON do not collide. 
- Implement stale-while-revalidate behavior for D1 components responses by returning stale entries immediately and scheduling a background refresh with `ctx.waitUntil` (`refreshStaleD1ComponentsEntry`). 
- Add two integration tests that verify serving cached D1 components HTML and JSON from KV (`cf-proxy/test/worker.test.ts`).

### Testing
- Ran `bun test cf-proxy/test/worker.test.ts` which initially errored due to a missing `kysely-d1` package in the test environment, then installed dependencies with `bun install` and re-ran the tests. 
- After installing deps, `bun test cf-proxy/test/worker.test.ts` passed (all new and existing integration tests passed). 
- Type-checked the project with `bunx tsc --noEmit` which succeeded. 
- Ran formatter with `bun run format` which completed successfully.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_b_69cc18ec85fc832eb2d9025de9224c84)

title: This PR updates the CLI docs to cover several `tsci` options that were missing or incomplete across the command reference pages.
description: This PR updates the CLI documentation to include missing tsci options and corrects existing descriptions, enhancing the clarity and completeness of the command reference pages.
body:
## Summary

This PR updates the CLI docs to cover several `tsci` options that were missing or incomplete across the command reference pages.

## What changed

- added missing `tsci build` flags, including:
  - `--routing-disabled`
  - `--pcb-png`
  - `--3d-png` and clarified `--3d` as its alias
  - `--show-courtyards`
  - `--kicad-project-zip`
  - `--inject-props`
  - `--inject-props-file`
- fixed and normalized some existing `tsci build` option descriptions
- documented `--show-courtyards` for `tsci export`
- added an example for exporting PCB SVGs with courtyard outlines
- documented additional `tsci snapshot` flags, including:
  - `--show-courtyards`
  - `--camera-preset`
  - `--ci`
  - `--test`
  - `--concurrency`

## Why

The command reference was missing several supported flags, which made the docs incomplete and harder to trust when using the CLI. This PR brings the docs closer to the actual interface and makes less-common but useful options easier to discover.

## Scope

Documentation only. No runtime or algorithmic changes.

title: Fix stale fixed-via integration by removing manual convex graph bootstrapping and adopting `FixedViaHypergraphSolver` auto-convex API
description: Removes manual convex graph bootstrapping in favor of the FixedViaHypergraphSolver auto-convex API for improved integration and reduced maintenance risk.
body:
This PR resolves an integration mismatch where `FixedTopologyHighDensityIntraNodeSolver` was still manually building convex via graphs while `fixed-via-hypergraph-solver` now supports one-call auto-convex setup.

### What changed
- Removed direct `createConvexViaGraphFromXYConnections` usage in intra-node routing.
- Switched initialization to `new FixedViaHypergraphSolver({ inputConnections })`.
- Removed obsolete `tiledViasByNet` state that was only needed for manual graph assembly.
- Updated `@tscircuit/fixed-via-hypergraph-solver` pin to latest merged commit:  
  `bed37a6201b5dd07c9cc68b828917ecc20d6d049`.

### Impact
- Aligns autorouter with the new upstream API.
- Reduces integration glue and duplicate graph-construction logic.
- Lowers maintenance risk by relying on the solver’s canonical internal convex/tile setup.

title: Fix obstacle pcb_port attribution in circuit-json conversion
description: Fixes incorrect attribution of pcb_port in circuit-json conversion by selecting the nearest pcb_port_id for obstacles instead of the first connected port, ensuring accurate DRC labels and alignment with obstacle geometry.
body:
Choose the nearest pcb_port_id for converted obstacles instead of taking the first pcb_port_* entry from connectedTo. 
This keeps pad and plated-hole attribution aligned with obstacle geometry and avoids misleading DRC labels when multiple ports are listed on the same obstacle metadata.

title: Fix zoom-induced dashed-trace rendering bug in `HighDensitySolver` by using scale-stable dash units
description: Fixes visualization issue where non-primary-layer traces appeared as near-continuous lines when zooming in by replacing fixed string dash pattern with numeric dash units and reducing non-primary-layer transparency for improved trace visibility.
body:
This PR fixes a visualization issue where non-primary-layer traces appeared as near-continuous lines when zooming in.

- Replaces fixed string dash pattern (`"10, 5"`) with numeric dash units (`[0.1, 0.3]`) for `segment.z !== 0`, so dash behavior remains consistent under zoom/scaling.
- Reduces non-primary-layer transparency (`safeTransparentize(..., 0.75 -> 0.5)`) to improve trace visibility while preserving top-vs-bottom layer distinction.
- Scope is visualization-only in `HighDensitySolver`; routing logic and geometry are unchanged.


### After

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e147336a-1b3e-4067-a03a-363400ff04c6


### Before 
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/349683bd-7e49-46f9-999f-acdc07f8108b

title: Reduce repair solver conflict geometry by collapsing collinear segment chains
description: Reduces the geometry complexity for conflict detection in the high-density repair solver by collapsing collinear segment chains into single segments, improving performance without altering route points or solver output.
body:
This speeds up the high-density repair solver by simplifying only the geometry used for conflict detection. Long same-layer collinear
  point chains are collapsed into single geometry segments in getRouteGeometry, while the actual route points and solver output remain
  unchanged.

  Benchmark on scripts/benchmark-first-1000.ts --limit 1000:

  - Before: 4696.31ms
  - After: 4297.13ms
  - Improvement: about 8.5%

title: speedup
description: Reduces benchmark time from 5078ms to 4581ms by optimizing conflict detection and move evaluation in the routing algorithm.
body:
- conflict detection now uses cached route bounds and cheap AABB pruning
  - single-route move evaluation skips redundant final conflict scans
  - benchmark moved from 5078ms to 4581ms on the first 1000 samples

title: Fix endpoint drift away from boundary edges in repair repros
description: Preserves route endpoints on any node boundary side they originally touch exactly, so repair moves no longer pull terminals off the graph edge in the cmn_72 and cmn_79 repros.
body: Preserves route endpoints on any node boundary side they originally touch exactly, so repair moves no longer pull terminals off the graph edge in the cmn_72 and cmn_79 repros.

title: Reduce solver overhead by skipping progress frame capture by default
description: Reduces solver overhead by skipping progress frame capture during normal solves and benchmarks, while allowing for explicit captureProgressFrames opt-in for full debugger visualization.
body:
This makes the solver faster by avoiding expensive intermediate frame cloning/building during normal solves and
  benchmarks, while preserving full debugger visualization behind an explicit captureProgressFrames opt-in. It also trims
  some queue overhead in movable/pushable index discovery.

  Benchmark on benchmark:first-1000 improved from 6220.06ms total / 6.22ms average to 5482.98ms total / 5.48ms average

title: Group solver profiling by solver name across scenarios, rank by total runtime, and improve sub-1s timing precision
description: Changes the solver profiling output to group by solver name, providing clearer metrics and improved timing precision.
body: This PR fixes misleading solver profiling output by changing the report from a split success/fail attempt view to a solver-level grouped view. ### What changed * Groups records by **solver name only** instead of `solver + success/fail` * Excludes **all pipeline wrapper solvers** via `startsWith("AutoroutingPipelineSolver")` * Adds clearer top-level metrics: * `Attempts` * `Scenarios` * `Success %` (scenario-level success rate) * `Total Time` * Changes ranking to **total accumulated runtime** so the biggest overall bottlenecks surface first * Increases sub-1000ms formatting precision from whole milliseconds to **2 decimal places** * Removes lower-value `P99` columns to keep the table focused on the most actionable metrics

title: Fix browser bundle stepts import resolution

title: fix: publish only dist and define stepts root exports explicitly
description: Fixes the package export structure to ensure that only the built artifacts are published, preventing internal paths from being exposed and causing import failures for downstream consumers.
body:
Motivation:
  Downstream browser/CDN consumers were resolving stepts in a way that exposed internal lib/... paths, which made root
  imports fragile and contributed to deep subpath resolution such as lib/core/EntityId. That internal path is not a stable
  published API surface and was causing downstream browser import failures.

  Fix:

  - point main and module to ./dist/index.js
  - add types for the built declaration entrypoint
  - add an explicit root exports map for "."
  - publish only dist so the package consistently exposes the built artifact used by consumers

  This makes the public package contract explicit and ensures import { ... } from "stepts" resolves through the built root
  entrypoint rather than the internal source layout.

title: Nearby obstacle-segment proximity check for high-density solver; enable for VertHorzLayer strategy and update tests
description: Adds a configurable proximity check to avoid routes coming too close to existing obstacle segments, improving DRC results and enabling it by default for the VertHorzLayer strategy.
body:
### Motivation
- Avoid routes that come too close (centerline-to-centerline) to existing obstacle segments even when they don't strictly intersect, preventing accidental contacts and improving DRC results.
- Make the proximity check configurable and enable it by default for the `VertHorzLayer_FutureCost` strategy with a sensible clearance tied to `traceThickness` and `obstacleMargin`.
- Update tests and snapshots to reflect the improved routing behavior and to assert absence of accidental-contact DRC errors for the affected dataset.

### Description
- Added configurable proximity-check flags and parameters to `SingleHighDensityRouteSolver`: `ENABLE_NEARBY_SEGMENT_PROXIMITY_CHECK` and `NEARBY_SEGMENT_CLEARANCE`, and constructor options `enableNearbySegmentProximityCheck` / `nearbySegmentClearance` to set them.
- In `doesPathToParentIntersectObstacle`, expanded the obstacle-index search box by the configured `clearance` and added a centerline distance test using a new helper `getSegmentToSegmentCenterlineDistance` to detect too-close segments in addition to intersections.
- Implemented the helper `getSegmentToSegmentCenterlineDistance` (uses existing `pointToSegmentDistance`) near other geometry utils.
- Enabled the proximity check by default in `SingleHighDensityRouteSolver6_VertHorzLayer_FutureCost` and set `nearbySegmentClearance` default to `(opts.traceThickness ?? 0.15) + (opts.obstacleMargin ?? 0.15)` when not provided.
- Updated tests and fixtures: modified `tests/bugs/circuit015-accidental-contact.test.ts` to assert zero accidental contacts after autorouting and updated multiple SVG snapshot files under `tests/__snapshots__` and `tests/features/__snapshots__` to match the new visualization output.

### Testing
- Ran the test suite including `tests/bugs/circuit015-accidental-contact.test.ts`, which now asserts `accidentalContacts.length === 0`, and it passed.
- Updated and validated SVG snapshot tests under `tests/__snapshots__` and `tests/features/__snapshots__`, and the rendering comparisons succeeded with the new snapshots.
- Overall automated tests (unit/e2e snapshots referenced above) completed successfully.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_b_69ce513d11c48328a02eb92189a3b0f2)

title: Add Delta column to benchmark PR details comparing PR vs main
description: Add a Delta column to benchmark results in PR details to indicate improvements or regressions compared to the main branch.
body:
### Motivation
- Provide quick per-sample visibility of regressions or improvements when `/benchmark` is run in a PR by comparing PR results to main.

### Description
- Update `.github/workflows/benchmark.yml` to compute an outcome score per test (`unsolved`=0, `solved w/ DRC fail`=1, `solved w/ DRC pass`=2) and build an index of main branch tests keyed by `solverName::scenarioName`.
- Add logic to compare each PR test to the corresponding main test and render a new `Delta` column with `✅` for improvements, `❌` for regressions, and an empty cell for unchanged or missing baselines.
- Show the `Delta` column only in the PR Details table while leaving the Main Branch Results rendering unchanged.

### Testing
- Ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` to typecheck the repository, which succeeded.
- Ran `bun run format` to apply formatting, which completed successfully.

title: Add /update-snapshots comment command workflow
description: Adds a GitHub Actions workflow that allows users to update test snapshots via a comment command in pull requests, automating the process of running tests and committing changes back to the PR branch.
body:
### Motivation
- Provide a one-step, CI-driven way to update test snapshots from a PR by running tests with snapshot-updates enabled and pushing resulting changes back to the PR branch.
- Reduce manual snapshot update friction and surface status in a PR comment so authors can see progress and results.

### Description
- Added a new workflow `.github/workflows/update-snapshots.yml` that triggers on PR comments starting with `/update-snapshots` or `/us` and requires the commenter be `OWNER`, `MEMBER`, or `COLLABORATOR`.
- The workflow posts a status comment, resolves the PR head ref and whether the runner can push (fork guard), checks out the PR branch with the bot token, sets up Bun, runs `bun install`, then runs `BUN_UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 bun test --timeout 120_000`, and if tests produce changes it stages and pushes the updated snapshots to the PR branch and reports the result in the status comment.
- Updated `.github/workflows/benchmark-instructions.yml` to document the new `/update-snapshots` (and `/us`) command and describe the `BUN_UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 bun test --timeout 120_000` behavior.

### Testing
- Ran `bun run format` and formatting completed successfully.
- Ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` and TypeScript type-checking passed.

------

title: Stitch degenerate fragmented MST connections as a single route set
description: Fixes autorouting issue where degenerate fragmented MST connections were incorrectly processed as separate jobs, leading to erroneous routing paths.
body: Merge per-island stitch jobs back into one stitch pass per connectionName before selecting the final endpoint path. This fixes bugreport44-0ec411, where source_net_0_mst0 was being stitched as two separate jobs and one of them bridged diagonally across the board to the global endpoint.

title: ci: automate npm releases and version bump PRs for @tscircuit/mm
description: Adds a GitHub Actions workflow to automate npm releases for tscircuitmm from main, reducing manual release steps and making the publish flow more consistent and repeatable.
body:
This PR adds a GitHub Actions workflow to automate npm releases for @tscircuit/mm from main.

  It builds the package with Bun, runs pver release --no-push-main, creates a version bump PR instead of pushing directly to main, enables auto-merge for the generated PR, supports optional upstream package update
  triggers, and notifies the release tracker after version updates.

  This reduces manual release steps and makes the publish flow more consistent and repeatable.

title: Prevent mmStr from emitting scientific notation for small metric values
description: Fixes a subtle formatting bug in mmStr where very small values could be returned in scientific notation instead of a plain decimal millimeter string.
body:
Description

  ## Summary

  This PR fixes a subtle formatting bug in mmStr where very small values could be returned in scientific notation (for example 1e-7mm) instead of a plain decimal millimeter string.

  mmStr now formats output with Intl.NumberFormat using standard notation, so callers consistently receive decimal strings like 0.0000001mm.

  ## Why This Change

  Scientific notation is awkward for downstream display, serialization, and user-facing output. Since mmStr is intended to produce a readable millimeter string, returning exponential notation breaks that
  expectation for very small values.

  ## Fix

  - Format mmStr output with Intl.NumberFormat
  - Disable grouping so values remain machine- and human-friendly
  - Force standard notation to avoid exponential output

  ## Reproduction And Tests

  This PR includes tests that reproduce the bug and verify the fix:

  - mmStr(1e-7) now returns 0.0000001mm
  - mmStr("0.0000001in") now returns 0.00000254mm

  ## Impact

  This is a subtle but important bug fix with test coverage, and it keeps mmStr output stable and readable for very small converted values.

title: Add support for autorouterVersion="v5"
description: Adds support for the autorouter version v5 in the TypeScript union, updates validation schemas, and regenerates documentation to reflect this new allowed value.
body:
### Motivation
- The autorouter version enum needs to include `v5` so consumers can specify the new autorouter runtime.
- Validation schemas and generated documentation must reflect the new allowed value to keep runtime and docs in sync.

### Description
- Added `"v5"` to the `autorouterVersion` TypeScript union in `lib/components/group.ts`.
- Updated the runtime Zod schema `subcircuitGroupProps` to accept `"v5"` (in `lib/components/group.ts`).
- Added a unit test in `tests/autorouter.test.ts` asserting `autorouterVersion: "v5"` parses correctly.
- Regenerated artifacts and docs so generated files reflect the change (`generated/COMPONENT_TYPES.md`, `generated/PROPS_OVERVIEW.md`, `README.md`).

### Testing
- Ran the generation scripts: `bun scripts/generate-component-types.ts`, `bun scripts/generate-manual-edits-docs.ts`, `bun scripts/generate-readme-docs.ts`, and `bun scripts/generate-props-overview.ts` (succeeded).
- Executed tests with `bun test tests/autorouter.test.ts` which passed: 8 tests, 0 failures.
- Type-checked with `bunx tsc --noEmit` (succeeded).
- Ran formatting with `bun run format` (succeeded).

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_b_69d1235b1638832eaef61a2124e76d8d)

title: Preserve imported footprint aliases and internal pin connectivity during inflation
description: Fixes the issue where imported footprint aliases are dropped when a matching port already exists, ensuring correct alias merging and treating numeric hints as valid pin identifiers.
body:
Issue reported by agent

Imported footprint aliases could be dropped when a matching port already existed, which made selectors like .A1 work in netlists but fail after inflation. Numeric footprint hints like "1" and "2" were also being treated as missing pin identifiers, causing incorrect alias merging onto the wrong pin.

  This change merges aliases into matching ports instead of discarding them, treats numeric hints as valid pin identifiers, and mirrors internally connected pin groups onto source_component.internally_connected_source_port_ids so downstream PCB connectivity checks see the same internal shorts. It also adds a regression covering inflated circuit-json aliases remaining selectable and routable.

title: Add autorouterVersion="v5" support and gated snapshot test
description: Expose the new high-density v5 autorouter pipeline so callers can opt into autorouterVersionv5 when running local autorouting.
body:
### Motivation
- Expose the new high-density v5 autorouter pipeline so callers can opt into `autorouterVersion="v5"` when running local autorouting. 
- Allow props validation to accept `v5` so component props and tests can opt into the new solver.

### Description
- Imported and exposed `AutoroutingPipelineSolver5` in the shared `SOLVERS` registry so it can be constructed by name via `SOLVERS[...]` in `lib/solvers.ts`.
- Extended `TscircuitAutorouter` types and selection logic in `lib/utils/autorouting/CapacityMeshAutorouter.ts` to accept `autorouterVersion?: "v5"` and route that value to `AutoroutingPipelineSolver5`.
- Added a feature test `tests/features/autorouter-version-v5.test.tsx` and generated the PCB snapshot `tests/features/__snapshots__/autorouter-version-v5-pcb.snap.svg`, and updated the test to `test.skip` with an inline comment explaining that v5 relies on a network-backed cache and can be flaky in CI/offline environments.
- Bumped `@tscircuit/props` dev dependency to `^0.0.502` in `package.json` so props validation/types accept `autorouterVersion="v5"`.

### Testing
- Ran `bun update --latest @tscircuit/capacity-autorouter` and `bun update --latest @tscircuit/props` to ensure runtime/type support for v5, which succeeded.
- Executed `BUN_UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 bun test tests/features/autorouter-version-v5.test.tsx` to run the v5 test once and create the PCB snapshot, which passed.
- Verified existing autorouter version tests with `bun test tests/features/autorouter-version-v1.test.tsx tests/features/autorouter-version-v3.test.tsx tests/features/autorouter-version-v4.test.tsx`, which all passed; then confirmed `bun test tests/features/autorouter-version-v5.test.tsx` reports the test as skipped.
- Ran typecheck and formatting with `bunx tsc --noEmit` and `bun run format`, which completed successfully.

------

title: Fix benchmark comment updates when benchmark workflow fails
description: Fixes the issue where the benchmark job fails early, preventing the temporary comment from being updated with failure details in the PR thread.
body:
### Motivation
- When the benchmark job failed early (for example during dependency installation) the temporary “Running benchmark…” PR comment was never updated and provided no failure details to the PR thread.

### Description
- Update the post-comment step condition to run on `always()` but only when a status comment was actually created by checking `steps.parse.outputs.status_comment_id`.
- Make result reading resilient by adding a `readText` helper and nullable fallbacks so missing `benchmark-result*.txt` files do not throw.
- Include `job.status` and a failure-aware title/body so the comment shows an explicit "Autorouting Benchmark Failed" message and a short explanation when the workflow did not succeed.
- Improve links by rendering the workflow run URL and using that for the artifact link in the updated comment; all changes are in `.github/workflows/benchmark.yml`.

### Testing
- Ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` which succeeded. 
- Ran `bun run format` which succeeded (formatted repository files).

------
